name: Graphile Worker PostgreSQL Job Queue
what_it_does: High-performance PostgreSQL-native job queue for Node.js — running background tasks directly in PostgreSQL using LISTEN/NOTIFY and SKIP LOCKED, eliminating the need for Redis, providing durable job processing with full ACID guarantees.
use_cases: Processing background jobs in Node.js applications without Redis infrastructure dependency
Scheduling and running recurring tasks with PostgreSQL as the single data store
Building durable job queues where jobs must survive server restarts and crashes
Running email sending, report generation, and data processing in background workers
Agent task queuing that leverages existing PostgreSQL infrastructure
not_for: Applications without PostgreSQL (use BullMQ for Redis or Celery for Python)
Sub-millisecond job processing requiring Redis-level queue performance
Distributed queues across multiple databases or message brokers
best_when: A Node.js application already uses PostgreSQL and needs durable background job processing without adding Redis infrastructure dependency.
avoid_when: You don't have PostgreSQL, need very high throughput (millions/minute), or prefer managed queue services.

auth.notes: Database URL for PostgreSQL connection — inherits PostgreSQL auth. No separate API auth. Database credentials control access. Standard PostgreSQL auth methods (password, SSL, IAM for cloud DBs).

pricing.free_tier_limits: MIT open source — completely free; no limits
pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tier — pure open source
pricing.notes: MIT open source — completely free. Only cost is PostgreSQL infrastructure (already present in most Node.js stacks). Widely used in production. Excellent performance for typical web application workloads.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — limited by PostgreSQL performance (typically thousands of jobs/sec)

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Library interface rather than REST API — agents integrate via Node.js SDK. Comprehensive documentation covering job patterns, retries, and scheduling. TypeScript types for type safety. SQL helpers for job management.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Job execution exactly-once guaranteed by PostgreSQL SKIP LOCKED. Job deduplication via unique job key. Failed jobs retry automatically with configurable backoff.
known_agent_gotchas: Node.js only — not suitable for Python, Go, or other language agents
PostgreSQL required — not compatible with MySQL, SQLite, or other databases
Worker must be running for jobs to execute — not fire-and-forget without worker process
Large job payloads stored in PostgreSQL — consider size limits for data in job arguments

security_score_components.security_notes: MIT open source — fully auditable. Security inherits from PostgreSQL — mature and battle-tested. TLS for PostgreSQL connections. No separate credentials — uses existing DB access control. Job data encrypted if DB encrypted.
